changelog: Two waves of fixes since v4.22.1. Wave 1 (commit 492919a, pushed without a version bump): (a) zone-classifier TEMPLATE_GLOBS used 'core/*.js' single-star, which silently skipped every file under core/_lib/ since the v4.20.x library split — targets upgrading from a pre-_lib release ended up with core/*.js refreshed but _lib/ missing entirely, then template-updater.js failed at require-time on the clean target. Fixed by widening both core and hooks globs to '**/*'; ALWAYS_SKIP_DIRS at the file-walker level continues to keep __tests__/ out. Added two regression tests. (b) memory-manager.initDb() leaked the SQLite file handle when `new DatabaseSync()` succeeded but `db.exec(CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE ... USING fts5)` threw (Node ships node:sqlite without FTS5 by default) — the catch block set this.db = null without closing the half-opened handle, surfacing on Windows as 23 EPERM failures during test tmp-dir cleanup and in production as undeletable memories.db after a failed init. Now close()+null in the catch. (c) Defense-in-depth maxRetries/retryDelay on test tmp-dir rmSync. Wave 2 (this version): added better-sqlite3 as an optionalDependency in .claude/core/package.json — it ships SQLite compiled with FTS5, the only backend where full-text memory search actually works. Resolution order in memory-manager.js: better-sqlite3 → node:sqlite (FTS5-less fallback) → JSON-only (no SQLite). All three paths share the same minimal API surface (new DatabaseSync(path), db.exec, db.prepare, stmt.run, db.close), so callers don't change. generateReport() now exposes sqliteBackend and sqliteSupportsFts5 in the storage section so it's visible which path is active. Tests: 1287 pass, 3 skipped, 0 failures.
